fields.description:
## Context
rebar's LOCAL ticket-store write path commits one git object graph per event (~3.3
commits/event on the `tickets` branch). This is the git-issue-style high-bloat pattern;
the durable way to bound growth is to create fewer objects at the source — the git-bug
`operationPack` pattern (multiple ops serialized -> one commit). Supersedes orphan e3d0
(sleek-bur-train) and folds duplicate 39c5 (grungy-grain-drawl). Discovered from the
closed bounded-git-growth epic 97e7, which addressed *reclaim* (stock gc) but left the
*growth rate* at write time out of scope.

**SCOPE (corrected — the original text was stale).** This epic is about the LOCAL ticket
store write path ONLY (`src/rebar/_store`). The Jira **reconciler**
(`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`) is OUT OF SCOPE — the original "WS-B3: reconciler
batch-apply" line was a mis-scoped artifact and is DROPPED. The reconciler's inbound path
(`inbound_translate._write_event_file` writing an event file under the lock without
committing, then `rebar_reconciler/__main__.py` batching via `commit_tree` + CAS
`update_ref`) is a REFERENCE that proves the batch pattern is safe in this codebase (same
store, same lock, same canonical bytes) — not something we modify. The new primitive lives
in `_store` and is index-based (atomic-rename + `git add` + one `git commit`), mirroring
the existing single-event `stage_and_commit`.

**Why batching is invariant-safe (verified).** Replay, dedup, union-merge convergence, and
SNAPSHOT compaction all key off each event's per-event UUID, NOT commit boundaries
(`reducer/_replay.py`, `_cache.py`, `_commands/compact.py`). So collapsing N commits into 1
is invisible to readers as long as each event stays its own uuid-named file (invariant I2)
and the single locked writer (I5) is preserved.

**Already shipped (do NOT rebuild).** The importer (`src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py`)
already has idempotency-by-`source_id` (`_scan_existing_source_ids`) and deferred-push (it
sets `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` and does one final push). What remains is that it still commits
one-per-event via `create_ticket`/`edit_ticket`/`link`/`comment`/`transition_compute`.

## Success Criteria
- A batch-commit write primitive exists in `_store` and is adopted on the importer's
  bulk path, demonstrably reducing commit count on a synthetic many-event import, with no
  regression to replay, dedup, union-merge convergence, or SNAPSHOT compaction.

## Workstreams
- **B1** — `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(items)`: acquire the write lock ONCE (it is NOT
  re-entrant, so B1 cannot loop `stage_and_commit`); for each `(ticket_id, event)`
  validate + atomic-rename its canonical-bytes temp into place + `git add`; then ONE
  `git commit`. Replicate the existing per-event unstage-on-failure safety for all N
  paths; batch atomicity is all-or-nothing per commit (documented). Add a batched
  `write_and_push` variant (one push after). Single-event path unchanged.
- **B2** — route the importer's INDEPENDENT passes (Pass 1 CREATE, Passes 2a–2d
  parents/links/file-impact/comments) through B1 in chunks (default ~256; consider a
  config key). Pass 2e (statuses) is STATEFUL (children-before-parents, `cascade=False`)
  → leave per-event.
- **B4** — before/after commit-count benchmark on a synthetic many-event import; confirm
  crash-resume: a crash mid-batch must leave either the whole batch's commit or none, and
  the `source_id` re-scan resumes cleanly (idempotency already works — verify batching
  doesn't break partial-batch recovery).
- **(optional stretch)** — cascade transition/claim batching (parent-first cascade emits
  one commit per ancestor; stateful, medium risk). Note only; build only if B1/B2 land
  easily.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(items)`: one lock acquire, N uuid-named event files, one commit; all-or-nothing rollback on failure; single-event write path unchanged
- [ ] Batched `write_and_push` variant (one push after the batch commit)
- [ ] Importer routes its independent passes (1, 2a–2d) through B1 in chunks; Pass 2e (statuses) stays per-event
- [ ] Commit-count reduction demonstrated on a synthetic many-event import (before/after benchmark)
- [ ] No regression: replay, dedup, cross-clone union-merge convergence, and SNAPSHOT compaction tests green
- [ ] Crash-resume (idempotent by source_id) test proves partial-batch recovery leaves whole-commit-or-none
fields.title: Write-time op batching — a batched-commit primitive for the LOCAL ticket store + bulk-path adoption
